steps.title: Find the contextual signal
steps.content: The text contrasts a misleading case ("only a few users") with the actual case ("over 8,000 users"). The blank describes whether 8,000 ratings is enough to feel confident in the average.

steps.title: Pick the precise word
steps.content:
The blank needs to describe 8,000 ratings as a quantity sufficient to trust the average — contrasted with "only a few users" being misleading. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — excessive: "an excessive number to feel confident" — excessive means too much; calling 8,000 ratings too many contradicts the passage's point that more ratings increase reliability. ✗
  • Choice B — adequate: "an adequate number to feel confident" — adequate means sufficient or enough, exactly describing 8,000 ratings as enough to trust the average. ✓
  • Choice C — accurate: "an accurate number to feel confident" — accurate describes correctness, not quantity; the passage contrasts few vs. many users, not correct vs. incorrect numbers. ✗
  • Choice D — insufficient: "an insufficient number to feel confident" — insufficient means not enough, directly contradicting the passage's claim that 8,000 ratings support confidence. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
